original: Roman Emperor Julian moves from Antioch with an army of 90,000 to attack the Sassanid Empire in a campaign that leads to his death
Persian scholar Naser Khosrow begins a seven-year Middle Eastern journey, which he later describes in his book "Safarnama"
3rd Lateran Council (11th ecumenical council) opens in Rome
Utrecht Governor Maarten van Rossum plunders The Hague
Smoking tobacco is introduced to Europe by Spanish physician Francisco Fernández
Betuwe joins the Union of Utrecht
Virginia enacts the first American temperance law
Battle of Jankau in Bohemia: Sweden defeats Holy Roman Emperor Ferdinand III
South Sea dike in Amsterdam breaks after storm

Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I, the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, and the Venetian Republic sign an agreement establishing the Holy League to prevent further expansion of the Ottoman Empire into Europe
Handel's "Jubilate" first performance in a public rehearsal at St. Paul's Cathedral, London
Mission San Francisco de la Espada, first of the San Antonio missions, reestablished by Spanish missionaries on the bank of the San Antonio River
First US religious journal, "The Christian History," is published in Boston
Jacobite troops leave Aberdeen
Antonio de Ulloa takes possession of Louisiana Territory from the French
King Stanislaus Augustus Poniatowski grants rights to Jews of Kovno
Thomas Townshend, 1st Viscount Sydney, is named President of the Board of Trade.
French troops are defeated by Austrian forces and Liège is recaptured
Amsterdam celebrates Revolution on the Dam; Square of Revolution
Treaty of Basel - Prussia ends war with France
First newspaper published in Australia the Sydney Gazette and New South Wales Advertiser by government printer and ex-convict George Howe
Dutch city of Leeuwarden forbids Jews to go to synagogues on Sundays
Monroe is 1st US President inaugurated on March 5th, because 4th was Sunday
First Burmese War: The British officially declare war on Burma
First continuous filibuster in the US Senate begins, as the Democratic minority attempt to run out the clock on a bill to establish a national bank, lasts until March 11
Over 500 Mexican troops led by Rafael Vasquez invade Texas, briefly occupy San Antonio and then head back to the Rio Grande.
Louis Antoine Garnier-Pages is named French minister of Finance
The Britannia Bridge across the Menai Strait between the Isle of Anglesey and the mainland of Wales is opened
Covent Garden Opera House, London, destroyed in a fire
Georgia becomes 1st state to regulate railroads
Parma, Tuscany, Modena and Romagna vote in referendums to join the Kingdom of Sardinia
Union troops under Brigadier General Wright occupy Fernandina, Florida (US Civil War)
1st track meet between Oxford & Cambridge
Arrigo Boito's opera "Mefistofele" premieres in Milan
Stapler patented in England by C H Gould
US Senate organizes to decide charges against President Andrew Johnson
Seattle authorizes 1st municipal employment office in US
Italian governor of Eritrea, General Baldissera, reaches Massawa
Italian premier Francesco Crispi resigns
American Negro Academy forms
1st performance of Edward MacDowell's 2nd Concerto in D
Definitive treaty for construction of Baghdad railway drawn up
1st radio broadcast of a musical composition aired
The second Duma opens in St. Petersburg, Russia and 40,000 demonstrators have to be dispersed by Russian troops
1st ascent of Mt Erebus, Antarctica
Ramon Inclan's "La Farsa Infantil de la Cabeza del Dragon" premieres
Stanley Cup, Dey's Arena, Ottawa, ON: Montreal Wanderers beat Ottawa Senators, 3-1
Italian forces are the first to use airships for military purposes, using them for reconnaissance behind Turkish lines.
Spanish steamer "Principe de Asturias" sinks NE of Spain, 500 die
World War I: The LZ 33, a zeppelin, is damaged by enemy fire and stranded south of Ostend.
Louis Hirsch & Harold Atteridge's musical premieres in NYC
The Durban Land Alienation Ordinance passes, enabling the Durban City Council to exclude Indians from ownership or occupation of property in white areas, South Africa
The US warns Costa Rica and Panama to settle disputes peacefully
Montana & Nevada become 1st states to enact old age pension laws
Frank Carauna, becomes 1st to bowl 2 successive perfect 300 games
King Hussein of Hedzjaz appoints himself caliph, after the fall of the Ottoman Empire
1,000 US marines land in China to protect American property
Karl Zuckmayer's "Der Hauptmann von Köpenick" premieres in Berlin
Germany's Nazi Party wins majority in parliament (43.9%-17.2M votes)
Mother-in-law's day 1st celebrated (Amarillo, Texas)
1st premature baby health law in US (Chicago)
Spitfire makes its 1st flight (Eastleigh Aerodrome in Southampton)
Japanese troop march into Batavia
Anti-fascist strikes in Italy
RAF bombs Essen Germany
Allies bombs The Hague, Netherlands
GeneralsDwight D. Eisenhower, George S. Patton and Alexander Patch meet in Lunéville, France
US 7th Army Corps captures Cologne
World War II: The Battle of the Ruhr begins.
Hungarian Communists and Social Democrats co-found the Left Bloc.
US rocket flies record 4800 kph to 126km height
Jharkhand Party is founded in India to campaign for a separate Jharkhand state
WBBJ TV channel 7 in Jackson, TN (ABC) begins broadcasting
"King Kong" 1st televised
Sgt Bilko satirizes Elvis Presley (Elvis Pelvin)
Explorer 2 fails to reach Earth orbit
KDUH TV channel 4 in Scottsbluff-Hay Spring, NB (ABC) 1st broadcast
Iran & US sign economic & military treaty
Ice Dance Championship at Vancouver won by Denny & Jones (GRB)
Ice Pairs Championship at Vancouver won by Wagner & Paul (CAN)
The Aquatic Ape Hypothesis originates when Alister Hardy publicly announces his idea that ape-human divergence may have been due to a coastal phase.
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champions in Vancouver won by Carol E Heiss (USA)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
The Beatles record "From Me to You" and "Thank You Girl"
Atlanta Mayor Ivan Allen Jr, announces a baseball team is moving there
Emergency crisis proclaimed in Ceylon due to social unrest
1st performance ofWalter Piston's 8th Symphony (his last), by the Boston Symphony, conducted by Erich Leinsdorf
Ernie Terrellbeats Eddie Machen by unanimous points decision over 15 rounds in Chicago, Illinois to win vacant WBA heavyweight boxing title
March Intifada: A Leftist uprising erupts in Bahrain against British colonial presence
75 MPH air currents causes BOAC 707 crash into Mount Fuji, 124 die
Bob Seagren pole vaults 5.19m indoor world record
Player reps elect Marvin Miller as executive director of the Major League Baseball Players Association
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
WEDN TV channel 53 in Norwich, CT (PBS) begins broadcasting
US launches Solar Explorer 2 to study the Sun
Gold reaches then record high ($47 per ounce) in Paris
Gustav Heinemann elected President of West Germany
Joe Orton's "What the Butler Saw" premieres in London
Dubnium atoms are first detected conclusively.
Edison Theater opens at 240 W 47th St NYC
Nuclear non-proliferation treaty goes into effect
Dutch speed skater Atje Keulen-Deelstra becomes Women's Allround World Champion at Heerenveen, Netherlands, her second title in 3 years
Greek composer Mikis Theodorakis leaves communist party
Yankee pitchers Peterson & Kekich announce they swapped wives

First performance in 3,000 years of world's oldest known song "Hymn to Nikkal" a 3,400 year old Hurrian hymn to moon god Nikkal from Ugarit in Syria, played at Berkeley University by Anne Kilmer and Richard Crocker
Ralph Stewart failed in 2nd Islander penalty shot
British pound falls below $2 for 1st time
Landsat 3 launched from Vandenberg AFB, California
Iran resumes petroleum exports
Voyager I's closest approach to Jupiter (172,000 miles)
Earth satellites record gamma rays from remnants of supernova N-49
Ice Pairs Champ at Hartford won by Irina Vorobieva & I Lisovski (URS)
US government grants Atlanta $1 million to search for black boy murderer
Russian spacecraft Venera 14 lands on Venus sends back data
Supreme Court (5-4): city may use public money for Nativity scene
US accuse Iraq of using poison gas

About 700 grain farmers from America's Midwest rally in Washington, D.C. at the Agriculture Department and march to the White House, demanding higher guaranteed prices for their products in pending farm legislation
Mexican authorities find the body of US drug agent Enrique "Kike" Camarena Salaazar
"Today" tabloid launched (Britain's 1st national colour newspaper); it folded in 1995
Constitution of Turks and Caicos Islands is restored and revised.
19th Easter Seal Telethon raises $37,002,000
Elly Verhulst runs world record 3000 m indoor (8:33.82)
Iraq repealed its annexation of Kuwait
Reggie Miller (Indiana Pacers) begins NBA free throw streak of 52 games
Ethic committee votes to reveal congressmen who bounced checks
Fokker 100 crashes at Skopje Macedonia, 81 die
Marlins beat Astros 12-8 in their 1st spring training game
Jefffesron Airplane/Starship singer Grace Slick arrested for pointing a gun at a cop
Largest milkshake made; 1,955 gallons of chocolate in Nelspruit, South Africa
PBA National Championship won by David Traber
Estonia Centrumlinkse Coalition party wins parliamentary election
Paul Okalik is elected first Premier of Nunavut
Thirty-five Muslim pilgrims are crushed to death during the annual Hajj pilgrimage in Mecca
Seventeen Israeli civilians are killed by a Hamas suicide bomber in the Haifa bus 37 bombing
David Silva scores 38' winner as Manchester City scores 1-0 win over Wigan at City of Manchester Stadium to start EPL record 20 home game winning streak; streak ends 31/3/12
27 members of Iraq's security force are killed by gunmen disguised as police in Haditha
Tropical Storm Irina kills 65 in Madagascar
Dow Jones Industrial Average surpasses its 2007 pre-financial crisis levels for 1st time
Willcom announces the world’s smallest mobile phone, weighing 32 grams
A survey by the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights reports that about 1/3 of women in the European Union have experienced physical or sexual violence since the age of 15
-8th Islamic State militants ransack and destroy ancient cities of Nimrud, Hatra and Dur-Sharrukin in Iraq
US air strike kills 150 Al-Shabaab militants north of Mogadishu, Somalia
China announces a military budget of 1.11 trillion yuan ($175 billion)
Bugatti announces the most expensive new car ever made - the La Voiture Noire costing €16.7 million (almost $19 million), only one will be made
Japanese architect Arata Isozaki wins architecture's Pritzker Prize
Major study into the MMR vaccine involving over 650,000 children in Denmark finds it does not increase the risk of autism
Second-ever person "cured" of HIV after stem cell transplant treatment in London, England results published in "Nature"
Japanese architect Riken Yamamoto wins 2024 Pritzker Architecture Prize for buildings that are a "background and foreground to everyday life"
Computing's top prize, the Turing Award is given to American researchers Andrew Barto and Richard Sutton for their work on reinforcement learning, a cornerstone of AI technology
Manufacture of bone tools by ancient humans is pushed back a million years by the discovery of a 1.5 million year old cache of bone tools in Olduvai Gorge, Tanzania
English KingHenry VIIgrantsJohn Cabot(Giovanni Caboto) a commission to explore for new lands
Astronomical work 'de Revolutionibus' byNicolaus Copernicusplaced on Catholic Forbidden index
First American Shakespearean production, an altered Richard III, is performed in New York City

John Hancockdelivers the fourth annual Massacre Day oration, a commemoration of the Boston Massacre, and denounces the presence of British troops in Boston, enhancing Hancock's stature as a leading Patriot
1st performance ofLudwig van Beethoven's 4th Symphony in B
Samuel Colt's Patent Arms Manufacturing Company manufactures first pistol, 36-caliber "Texas" model, in Paterson, New Jersey
Zachary Taylorsworn in as 12th US President

American engineerGeorge Westinghousepatents triple air brake for trains

US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonis inaugurated for a second term
Gandhi& British viceroy Lord Irwin sign pact in London - marks end of the Civil Disobedience Movement in India

Terence Rattigan's stage drama "Deep Blue Sea" premieres in London
Elvis Presleymakes his 1st TV appearance on a local video broadcast of the popular radio program "Louisiana Hayride" from the Shreveport Municipal Auditorium in Shreveport, Louisiana

Capitol Records releases "Songs for Swingin' Lovers!", the tenth album by American singerFrank Sinatra: upbeat collection of pop tunes with jazz arrangements by Nelson Riddle became the first album ever to top the new UK Albums Chart
Eamon de Valera's Fianna Fail-party wins election in Ireland
Iconic photograph 'Guerrillero Heroico' taken ofChe Guevaraat a memorial service in Havana, by Cuban photographer Alberto Korda, possibly the most reproduced photo in history

Kylie Jenneris the world's youngest-ever billionaire at 21, according to Forbes

ExplorerErnest Shackleton's ship Endurance, which sank in 1915, is rediscovered after 107 years in excellent condition in the Weddell Sea, Antarctica
Caitlin Clarkrecords 30 points, 17 assists, and 10 rebounds in the Big Ten tournament final for Iowa against Ohio State
Shirt worn by (a very wet)Colin Firthin 1995 BBC-TV  adaption ofJane Austen's "Pride and Prejudice" sells for  £20,000 ($32,000) at a charity auction in London

British soldiers kill five men in a crowd throwing snowballs, stones, and sticks at them. African AmericanCrispus Attucksis the first to die and is later held up as an early Black martyr. The Boston Massacre or Incident on King Street galvanizes anti-British feelings.
Piano company Steinway & Sons is founded by Heinrich Steinweg (later Henry Steinway) in New York City
